name: Mission Dolores Park
note: Archive documents linked to this place; source labels honour the SFPL-vs-Democracy's-Library distinction.

documents.title: Minutes
documents.creator: San Francisco Open Space/Park Renovation Citizens Advisory Committee
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: Open Space/Park Renovation advisory-committee minutes weighing a $25,000 beautification, erosion-control and irrigation request for the park's west end, backed by Friends of Dolores Park.

documents.title: FY 2008-09 parks annual report : parks scores citywide increased for fourth year, but disparities remain between districts
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Office of the Controller. City Services Auditor Division,San Francisco (Calif.). Recreation and Park Dept
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: The Controller's FY2008-09 parks report ranking Mission Dolores Park tenth-lowest citywide at 74.6%, down 11.8 points, and noting it was flagged for capital work in the 2008 bond.

documents.title: Park standards, 6-month report FY 2009-10 : park scores citywide increased through the first half of FY 2009-10
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Office of the Controller. City Services Auditor Division
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: The Controller's FY2009-10 mid-year report showing the park dropping to 67.5%, second-lowest citywide.

documents.title: 2008 Clean & Safe Neighborhood Parks bond
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.)
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: The 2008 parks bond's project page for Mission Dolores Park — 14.2 acres, tennis and basketball courts, a sports field, playground and clubhouse — scoping its renovation.

documents.title: General manager's report : Open Space Acquisition and Park Renovation Fund
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Recreation and Park Department,San Francisco (Calif.). Dept. of City Planning,San Francisco (Calif.). Open Space Acquisition and Park Renovation Fund
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1975 Open Space fund report allocating $70,000 for children's play apparatus at Mission Dolores Park, with later safety-lighting funds.
